Domaine du Vissoux (Pierre-Marie Chermette)Beaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Easygoing · Funky

Domaine Jean-Claude LapaluBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Lapalu's entry-level Beaujolais AOC: natural fermentation in concrete gives a vibrant, crunchy red-fruit profile with racy acidity and a sauvage lift.

Beaujolais Nouveau

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Easygoing

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Beaujolais Nouveau is the widely-distributed reference for the third-Thursday-November release: carbonic-maceration Gamay bottled and released on the third Thursday of November, with characteristically exuberant fruit and soft structure.

Domaine Karim VionnetBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

P'tit Caillou ('little stone') is Vionnet's entry-level Beaujolais AOC: unpretentious, fresh Gamay with crunchy red fruit and mineral granite lift for immediate drinking.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Easygoing · Funky

Domaine Julien SunierBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Wild Soul is Sunier's entry-level Beaujolais AOC: funky, crunchy dark fruit and wild-herb energy from whole-cluster carbonic fermentation in cement, no additions.

Cuvée page →

Aromes de Pavie

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Originally the second wine of Pavie, now an independent brand since 2018 sourced from a dedicated 11-hectare east-plateau parcel. Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend of medium body with dark cherry, espresso and salty-mineral character; approachable on release.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Rouge

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's premium red from a single Bordeaux Superieur block: 75% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon aged in small barrels for added structure. Anne Krebiehl MW describes it as juicy, plush, and beautifully open with oak-kissed fruit.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Bordeaux Superieur

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's entry-level claret; a straightforward Merlot-Cabernet blend offering reliable, juicy red-fruit character and soft tannins at an accessible price. Drink now or hold for up to seven years. The wine is consistently good for its level.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Sauvignon Blanc

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Bauduc's benchmark white and most popular wine: 100% Sauvignon Blanc, stainless steel fermented for maximum freshness and varietal definition. Crisp, dry, and food-versatile; house white at Rick Stein's fish restaurants for over a decade.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Lariveau

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Moulin Pey-LabrieBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Merlot Fronsac from the 4.5-hectare Haut-Lariveau parcel acquired by the Hubau family in 1990. More approachable and fruit-forward than its Canon-Fronsac sibling, farmed with the same biodynamic discipline.

Cuvée page →

Chateau de la Riviere

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship Fronsac estate red from 60 hectares on clay-limestone soils above the Dordogne. Merlot-led and supple with dark chocolate and ripe cherry; aged in spectacular 16th-century underground cellars at constant 13 degrees.

Cuvée page →

Château Larose-Trintaudon

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The largest single vineyard in the Médoc produces an consistently approachable, fruit-forward Haut-Médoc with a well-balanced structure and excellent everyday drinkability at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Croix Canon

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau CanonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Canon (formerly Clos Canon, rebranded Croix Canon from the 2012 vintage). Elegant, medium-bodied with mint, cherry and floral character; structured enough for 10+ years but designed for earlier pleasure.

Cuvée page →

Echo de Lynch-Bages

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Lynch-Bages since 2007 (previously sold as Chateau Haut-Bages Averous). Higher Merlot component than the grand vin provides accessible fruit and earlier drinking; retains characteristic Lynch-Bages tobacco and cassis signature.

Cuvée page →

Fleur de Clinet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Clinet from estate vines, made to reveal its qualities early. Smooth-textured, fruit-driven Pomerol Merlot with approachable tannins and genuine ageing potential without requiring long cellaring.

Cuvée page →

L'Héritage de Chasse-Spleen

3.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The Haut-Médoc AOC second-level wine of Chasse-Spleen, made from satellite vineyard parcels. Easy-drinking, herb-inflected red fruit for everyday occasions.

Cuvée page →

L'Oratoire de Chasse-Spleen

3.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The approachable second wine of Chasse-Spleen from the Moulis AOC: Merlot-driven, with enticing red fruit and soft tannins, ready to enjoy without extended cellaring.

Cuvée page →

La Closerie de Camensac

3.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château de CamensacBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Château Camensac, prioritising freshness and supple tannins from younger vine selections. Light and immediately appealing with characteristic Haut-Médoc menthol freshness.

Cuvée page →

La Demoiselle de Sociando-Mallet

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Sociando-MalletBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The Merlot-dominant second wine of Sociando-Mallet, offering earlier approachability with a more typically Médoc fruit profile than the austere and structured grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Gravette de Certan

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Vieux Chateau CertanBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible, Merlot-dominant second selection from Vieux Chateau Certan, made from younger vines and less-selected barrels. Forward, fruit-driven, and approachable early but retaining the estate's hallmark iron-mineral signature.

Cuvée page →

La Réserve de Léoville Barton

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Léoville BartonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

An elegant and approachable expression of Saint-Julien from younger vines, with a higher Merlot proportion giving early accessibility and soft ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Le Blanc de Chateau de la Riviere

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

An unusual Bordeaux Blanc built on Sauvignon Gris rather than Semillon, giving tropical and citrus character with a rich, textured palate. Only 5,600 bottles produced annually; fermented mostly in stainless steel to preserve freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Carillon de l'Angelus

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Angelus, Merlot-dominant with a forward, polished character. Bright red fruit, soft spice and floral tones make it approachable younger than the grand vin while retaining the estate's Merlot generosity.

Cuvée page →

Le Clementin du Pape Clement Rouge

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Pape Clement since 1986. Merlot-dominant for approachability; round red cherry and currant fruit with cedar and soft spice notes. Designed for earlier drinking than the grand vin while reflecting the same Pessac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Le Lys de Lafaurie-Peyraguey

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Lafaurie-PeyragueyBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

A dry white Bordeaux introduced in 2014 from the Lafaurie-Peyraguey estate in Bommes. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant with Semillon adding body; white flowers, lemon blossom, and fresh citrus lead a mineral, herb-tinged palate. An accessible entry into the Sauternes estate's terroir as a dry expression.

Cuvée page →

Le Pauillac de Chateau Latour

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Third wine of Latour produced since 1989. Merlot-dominant and made primarily from young vines; developed for the restaurant market as a generously fruity, earlier-drinking Pauillac.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Easygoing

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Cheval Blanc: same terroir and typicity as the grand vin but in a more accessible, forward style. Charming black-fruit and cedar profile, silky tannins, and an earlier drinking window.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Blanc

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

The second white wine of Smith Haut Lafitte. Sauvignon Blanc-driven with Semillon adding roundness; lime tree, verbena, and citrus oil aromas lead a fresh, floral palate with elderflower and white peach. Vibrant acidity makes it an excellent aperitif and shellfish partner.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Rouge

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Smith Haut Lafitte, sharing the estate's Martillac gravel terroir. A Cabernet-Sauvignon-led blend with cassis, fresh herbs, and pencil-shaving notes in an approachable medium-bodied style suitable for earlier drinking.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Lion du Marquis de Las Cases

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Château Léoville-Las CasesBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The official second wine of Léoville-Las Cases since 2007, Merlot-dominant for earlier accessibility while retaining the Saint-Julien precision of the parent estate.

Cuvée page →

Les Griffons de Pichon Baron

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau Pichon-Longueville BaronBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Premium second wine of Pichon Baron introduced with the 2012 vintage. Merlot-dominant versus the Cabernet-heavy grand vin; since 2020, partially aged in amphora to add minerality and texture without excessive oak.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Pontet-Canet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pontet-Canet since 1982. Produced in limited quantities as Alfred Tesseron prefers lower yields and declassification over large second-wine production; earlier-drinking, approachable Pauillac from biodynamic-certified vineyards.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Trintaudon

3.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Larose-Trintaudon, sourced from the same vines and terroir as the grand vin but crafted for early approachability and everyday enjoyment.

Cuvée page →

Les Pagodes de Cos

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Cos d'Estournel since 1994; previously sold as Marbuzet. Shows a more Merlot-forward profile than the grand vin, with the estate's distinctive spice character in a more accessible format.

Cuvée page →

Mondot

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Troplong Mondot, Merlot-dominant with emphasis on freshness and early drinkability. A juicy, earthy black cherry and spring flower profile with wet-stone limestone terroir character; approachable and unpretentious.

Cuvée page →

Petit Becot

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Beau-Sejour BecotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The renamed second wine of Beau-Sejour Becot (formerly Tournelle de Beau-Sejour Becot), renamed Petit Becot in 2012. Merlot-dominant with fresh red cherry character and gentle tannin; designed for early drinking pleasure.

Cuvée page →

Petit-Figeac

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Easygoing

Chateau FigeacBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Chateau Figeac, one of the oldest second wines on the Right Bank (first produced 1945). Shares the same Cabernet-influenced gravel terroir and blending trio as the grand vin but from younger vines; lighter, more refined and more accessible earlier.

Cuvée page →

Prieur de Meyney

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Meyney since 1979, named for the estate's 17th-century priory origins. Produced from younger vines (approximately 35-40 years) using minimal new oak to preserve fresh fruit character; a reliable everyday Saint-Estephe.

Cuvée page →

R de Rieussec

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau RieussecBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

The dry white wine of Chateau Rieussec, a relative newcomer first produced in 1999. A fresh, citrussy Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from the Fargues-de-Langon terroir with herbal and mineral freshness; lighter-styled than the Sauternes grand vin but sharing its Semillon richness.

Cuvée page →

Reserve de la Comtesse

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de LalandeBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pichon Lalande since 1973. Described by the estate as a true Pauillac brand in its own right. Higher Petit Verdot proportion adds structure and spice; the estate's characteristic silk texture is detectable even at this level.

Cuvée page →

Gaston Chiquet Tradition Brut 1er Cru

★★★★★★★★★★

Champagne Gaston ChiquetChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Gaston Chiquet's Premier Cru house brut: playful and zesty with mandarin and lemon complementing red fruit, an affable, forward style with fresh acidity.

★★★★★★★★★★

Champagne LansonChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Lanson's signature non-malolactic house brut: citrus and green apple over white flowers and a touch of honey, the palate crisp and racy with a mineral cut.

Moet & ChandonChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

The most widely poured Champagne in the world: a bright, fruit-led brut of green apple and citrus with a brioche edge and an approachable, lively palate.

Mumm's red-sash house brut: citrus and white peach over a touch of caramel, the palate lively and fresh with fine bubbles and a clean finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Low acidity · Easygoing · Festive

Bodegas Gutiérrez ColosíaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Cream sherry from the Gutiérrez Colosía riverside bodega under the Mari Pepa label; medium-sweet, caramel-toned and approachable with the Atlantic character of El Puerto.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Easygoing

Delgado ZuletaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Mid-tier Amontillado from Delgado Zuleta at the accessible entry below VORS; dry, nutty, with the characteristic Sanlúcar saline freshness from the Manzanilla origin.

Napoleón Amontillado

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Easygoing

Bodegas Hidalgo La GitanaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Sanlúcar Amontillado retaining the Atlantic saline character of the Manzanilla base even after full biological-to-oxidative progression; dry, nutty and fresh.

Papirusa Manzanilla

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Emilio LustauJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Pale and delicate Manzanilla from Sanlúcar, shaped by Atlantic breezes and a thick flor veil. Saline, camomile and crisp lemon zest with a clean briny finish.

An accessible Langhe Nebbiolo from the Treiso estate, offering the grape's perfume and red fruit in an early-drinking, fairly priced style.

Ceretto Blange Langhe Arneis

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

CerettoPiedmont€€€€€€€Still White

Ceretto's pioneering Blange is a lightly textured Langhe Arneis with a faint spritz, one of the wines that revived the grape and a modern Piedmont staple.

Cuvée page →

G.D. Vajra Dolcetto d'Alba

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€Still Red

A benchmark Vajra Dolcetto, juicy and purple-fruited with the soft tannin and easy drinkability that define the everyday red of the Langhe.

Cuvée page →

G.D. Vajra Langhe Nebbiolo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€€Still Red

Vajra's Langhe Nebbiolo is a fresh, fragrant and early-drinking introduction to the grape, drawn from younger vines around Vergne.

Cuvée page →

Pecchenino Dogliani San Luigi

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

PeccheninoPiedmont€€€€€€Still Red

A red-purple Dogliani with violet nuances, blackberry, raspberry, blueberry and red cherry with mineral notes, medium-bodied with soft tannins, moderate acidity and good balance.

Produttori del BarbarescoPiedmont€€€€€€€Still Red

An accessible, earlier-drinking Langhe Nebbiolo from the cooperative's member vineyards, declassified from Barbaresco fruit.

★★★★★★★★★★

ViettiPiedmont€€€€€€€Still Red

A purplish-red Dolcetto with subtle violet hues, blackberry, raspberry and violet aromas and mineral, blueberry notes, medium-bodied with soft tannins and good balance.

ViettiPiedmont€€€€€€€Still Red

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bodegas VizcarraRibera del Duero€€€€€€€Still Red

Refreshing Crianza from Mambrilla de Castrejon at 900 metres altitude, where Juan Carlos Vizcarra achieves freshness and balance rare at this elevation in the Burgos sector.

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Cuvée page →

Adami Bosco di Gica Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Bosco di Gica (Gica's wood) is a mid-range Brut from Adami, positioned between Dei Casel and the Rive Giardino. The Brut style emphasises the Valdobbiadene zone's fresh, mineral character without the Ex Dry sweetness.

Cuvée page →

Adami dei Casel Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry-level Extra Dry from Adami. Dei Casel (meaning 'of the hamlet') has been the house's everyday accessible label since the early decades of production. Consistent, clean and approachable.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Palazzo della Torre

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

A Veronese IGT using partial ripasso over Amarone pomace adding richness to the Corvina-Rondinella blend, while a small Sangiovese component adds acidity. Approachable but complex mid-tier red from Allegrini.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Allegrini's estate Classico Superiore reflects the house's high Corvinone proportion (45%) adding floral lift and softer tannin alongside Corvina's cherry-herb character; a bright, food-friendly everyday Valpolicella.

Cuvée page →

Bele Casel Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bele CaselVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Asolo Prosecco DOCG is produced on the south-facing slopes of the Asolo hills, southwest of Valdobbiadene. Bele Casel uses indigenous Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera for a more complex aromatic profile than monovarietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bertani Valpolicella Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

BertaniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Bertani's Ripasso benefits from the estate's traditional Corvina-Rondinella blend and refermentation over dried Amarone skins, producing a mid-weight red with the freshness typical of the traditional house style.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Crede Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship multi-varietal Valdobbiadene Brut from Bisol. 'Crede' refers to the clay-limestone (crete) soils of the Valdobbiadene hills. Inclusion of indigenous Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta adds aromatic complexity absent in single-varietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Jeio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Jeio is the accessible everyday Brut tier from Bisol 1542, positioned below the premium Crede multi-varietal and the Rive expressions. The Glera-dominant blend with minor Verdiso and Bianchetta adds a touch of aromatic complexity to a clean, food-friendly Brut.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Relio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Extra Dry in Prosecco parlance is slightly sweeter (12-17 g/l residual sugar) than Brut. Relio is a soft, fruit-forward style with rounded acidity, suited to lighter food pairings and aperitivo moments.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Bandarossa Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Fruity · Easygoing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Bandarossa (red ribbon) is a fruitier, more aromatic Extra Dry than Senior. Designed for pairing with fruit-forward desserts and aromatic preparations, it expresses the warmer side of Valdobbiadene's Glera.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Senior Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship Extra Dry from Bortolomiol, founded in 1949 by Giuliano Bortolomiol. Named 'Senior' in tribute to the founder, this is a classic off-dry Valdobbiadene DOCG with the balance of sweetness and freshness that made the Extra Dry category popular.

Cuvée page →

Ca' La Bionda Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ca' La BiondaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Ca' La Bionda's Classico Superiore reflects the upper Marano valley's mineral character in a lighter-bodied, tart cherry register; crisp, food-friendly and genuinely typical of the traditional Valpolicella style.

Cuvée page →

Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Cantina Valpolicella NegrarVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The cooperative's estate Amarone Classico provides accessible quality from 230 family growers across the Classico zone; a solid, tobacco-cherry traditional Amarone at significantly better value than single-estate bottlings at comparable quality.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Custoza Bianco di Custoza DOC

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Cantina di CustozaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The cooperative's flagship Bianco di Custoza is the appellation's most accessible, high-volume expression. Consistent, clean and approachable, it represents good-value Custoza DOC at the walk-in tourist tier. The five-variety blend from cooperative members' morainic vineyards gives a typical zonal character.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The cooperative's entry-level Soave Classico DOC provides excellent access to the appellation's almond-blossom Garganega character at very accessible price; clean, fresh and food-versatile.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Rocca Sveva is the flagship quality expression from the Cantina di Soave cooperative; a consistent, well-made Garganega-dominant Superiore DOCG that provides excellent value access to the appellation's almond-mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Bardolino Classico DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

One of Bardolino's oldest estates, founded in 1889. Campagnola's Bardolino Classico is a genuine expression of the pale ruby, low-tannin, cherry-scented style from the original lakefront Bardolino zone. The estate's daily opening policy makes it a popular stop on the Lake Garda wine tourist circuit.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Valpolicella Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Campagnola's Valpolicella Classico from the estate's hillside vineyards in the historic Classico zone. A traditional expression of the DOC: light in colour and body but with the characteristic Corvina sour cherry and almond character of the Classico hills.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Bardolino Chiaretto Classico

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€Still Rose

Cavalchina's Chiaretto Classico extends the estate's Lake Garda portfolio from white to rosato. Brief skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara produces a pale copper-pink wine with fresh berry and rose aromatics; dry and food-versatile.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Bianco di Custoza

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Piona family's benchmark Bianco di Custoza. The DOC requires a minimum of five grape varieties, and Cavalchina's multi-varietal blend from morainic glacial soils south of Lake Garda delivers a crisp, food-friendly white with genuine complexity from the variety mix.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry Brut Valdobbiadene DOCG from Col Vetoraz. The estate's altitude above Santo Stefano produces a distinctly cool-climate Glera with sharper acidity and finer aromatics than lower-valley peers.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Extra Dry expression from Col Vetoraz's elevated hilltop estate. Off-dry with gentle floral sweetness; the altitude of the estate prevents the Extra Dry from tipping into overripe territory, maintaining freshness.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Bardolino Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Corte Gardoni's Bardolino Classico is produced from Corvina-dominated vines on the southern Garda morainic soils. Light-bodied, pale ruby and fresh: a classic everyday Garda red suited to fish, vegetable dishes and outdoor drinking.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Piccoli family's Bianco di Custoza from the morainic rim at Valeggio sul Mincio. The glacially deposited soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda moraine produce a fresh, food-friendly white with characteristic saline mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Drusian Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Drusian is the flagship Extra Dry from Foss Marai, named after the locality of the Guia vineyards. Softer and more aromatic than the Brut, with white peach and acacia blossom in an off-dry, easy-drinking style.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The house Brut from Foss Marai at Guia di Valdobbiadene. The Guia area has some of the steepest terraced vineyards in the DOCG zone; the Brut reflects this with a crisp, mineral precision.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Gini's estate Soave Classico 100% Garganega captures the family's 17th-century vine heritage in a fresh, mineral, everyday expression; the racy acidity and almond-blossom purity of the volcanic Monteforte basalt terroir at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Bardolino Classico DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Bardolino Classico from vineyards along the eastern Garda shore. A light, fresh Corvina-Rondinella red for everyday drinking, complementing the estate's main Custoza white wine focus.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Gorgo estate at Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio produces one of the most consistently mineral Bianco di Custoza expressions. The morainic glacial soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda amphitheatre impart a saline mineral quality to the Garganega-Trebbiano-Cortese blend.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Merlot Garda DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot provides a red wine counterpart to the estate's primary Bianco di Custoza focus. Lake Garda's moderating effect on the climate produces a well-rounded Merlot with soft tannins and ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Bardolino Classico

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

A classic light-bodied Bardolino Classico from the Lake Garda shoreline estates. The Classico denomination requires production from the original Bardolino historic core. Guerrieri Rizzardi, as one of the oldest estates in the zone, produces a benchmark expression of the pale ruby, cherry-scented style.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Il Tralcetto Pinot Grigio delle Venezie

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The volume accessible white from Italo Cescon. Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is the broad appellation for Pinot Grigio from the Tre Venezie; the Cescon version is produced from Piave valley floor vineyards and represents the commercial, food-versatile tier of the estate alongside the character-driven Raboso.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Merlot Piave DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Piave plain Merlot DOC from Italo Cescon is a classic Trevisan everyday red. Merlot on the alluvial Piave soils produces a plum-forward, medium-bodied wine with soft tannins suited to simple food pairings.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Le Salette's Classico Superiore benefits from the high-altitude Fumane terroir that gives freshness and floral lift to the Corvina base; an elegant, food-friendly expression of the appellation.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore from Ca' Bianca old vines shows the same mineral-iron freshness as the estate's Amarone in a lighter register; a food-friendly expression of traditional San Pietro in Cariano Corvina.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Pino and Toi Breganze Bianco

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pino and Toi (Pine and You in Veneto dialect) is Maculan's accessible dry white, a refreshing blend of the two Pinots and Tocai for everyday drinking. It represents the lighter, approachable side of the Breganze DOC in contrast to the estate's intense dessert wines.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

Cuvée page →

Monte Tondo Soave Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Monte TondoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Monte Tondo's entry-level Soave Classico DOC from vineyards within the historic Classico zone. A clean, accessible Garganega-Trebbiano blend for everyday drinking; lighter than the Casette Foscarin Superiore.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Nicolis estate Classico Superiore offers an honest, traditional Valpolicella from the Villa Girardi hillside; cherry-tobacco character with the freshness typical of a classic-zone Corvina aged gently in large casks.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Lugana DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Ottella's standard Lugana DOC is produced from Turbiana (the local name for Verdicchio in the Lugana DOC zone) from estate vineyards at Peschiera del Garda on the southeastern shore of Lake Garda. The clay-limestone soils of the Lugana plain give Turbiana its distinctive almond mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pieropan's estate Soave Classico is the reference-standard expression of the appellation: Garganega's characteristic almond-blossom and white-peach aromas with a saline, mineral finish from the volcanic basalt hillside soils of the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi San Vincenzo

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€Still White

San Vincenzo is Anselmi's most widely distributed wine, a Garganega-Chardonnay Veneto IGT that captures the almond-blossom freshness of the Soave zone in a light, food-friendly, affordable white. The Chardonnay adds body without altering the Garganega character.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Giustino B Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The historical benchmark of Ruggeri, first produced when the house was founded in 1950. Giustino B is named after Giustino Bisol, who co-founded the house; this Extra Dry expression became one of the most recognised Prosecco Superiore labels in the zone.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Oro Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri Oro is the accessible volume entry-level Extra Dry, designed for everyday aperitivo occasions. Consistent, clean and fruit-forward with the classic Prosecco Superiore profile.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Quartese Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri's brut style for occasions calling for a leaner, drier Prosecco Superiore than the classic Extra Dry. Quartese is named after a Valdobbiadene locality and emphasises the zone's mineral freshness.

Cuvée page →

Santa Sofia Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Santa SofiaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Sofia's Classico Superiore is a reliable, food-friendly, accessible Valpolicella from the historic Pedemonte estate with cherry-tobacco character and good acidity.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Bollicine di Prosecco

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Prosecco DOC entry point from the Montello red-wine estate. Serafini and Vidotto produces Bollicine di Prosecco from Treviso-zone Glera as an accessible sparkling alongside the estate's main red wine mission.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Phigaia

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Phigaia is the accessible second wine from Serafini and Vidotto, allowing the estate to express the Montello terroir at a lower price point. The Cabernet Franc and Merlot blend is lighter, fresher and more immediately enjoyable than Il Rosso dell'Abazia.

Cuvée page →

Speri Valpolicella Classico Superiore La Roverina

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Speri's La Roverina Classico Superiore with partial ripasso technique; fresh cherry and tobacco character from the traditional large-cask approach, with added concentration from the ripasso step and the estate's characteristic acidity.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's estate Soave Classico is a fresh, mineral, everyday expression of Garganega from the village of Soave; classic almond-blossom aromatics with a crisp, saline finish perfect for aperitivo or seafood.

Cuvée page →

Tedeschi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Capitel dei Nicalo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tedeschi ViniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

The Capitel dei Nicalo is Tedeschi's Valpolicella Superiore partially ripassoed over dried skins, offering accessible Corvina cherry fruit with tobacco structure and good food-pairing versatility.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tenuta Sant'Antonio's Ripasso shows the Mezzane valley's characteristic earthiness alongside cherry-tobacco fruit; the small Oseleta addition adds a distinguishing spice note that sets it apart from Classico-zone examples.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tommasi's Ripasso offers the hallmark refermentation complexity over dried Amarone skins in a well-structured, cherry-tobacco dominated medium red that is one of the zone's best-value offerings in the Ripasso DOC.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Pinot Bianco Colli Euganei

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pinot Bianco on the volcanic soils of the Euganean Hills produces a wine of notable mineral freshness. The volcanic basalt structure of the Colli Euganei translates into a flinty mineral quality in white wines rarely seen in the Veneto plains.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Il Fresco Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Villa Sandi's volume Prosecco DOC expression. Il Fresco is the globally distributed everyday label from the Palladian villa estate in Crocetta del Montello; consistent, approachable and affordable at the DOC level.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Valpolicella Superiore Ripassa

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Zenato's Ripassa shows the Oseleta grape's peppery-spice influence even at the mid-tier level: good concentration from refermentation over dried skins with a distinguishing warmth from the native Oseleta component.
